label: Observe–plan–act agent loops

node.label: Observe–plan–act agent loops
node.definition: An observe–plan–act loop maintains state, selects tools or actions, processes feedback, and iterates until a goal, budget, or safety condition is reached.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Observe–plan–act agent loops is to connect Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Tool use & function calling to downstream capabilities including Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets, Skill libraries, workflows & procedural plans.
examples: substrate for Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
substrate for Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets
substrate for Skill libraries, workflows & procedural plans
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Tool use & function calling.

dependencies.rationale: Observe–plan–act agent loops uses Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Observe–plan–act agent loops uses Tool use & function calling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Observe–plan–act agent loops stores, selects, or obtains information through Context windows & working memory.
Observe–plan–act agent loops uses Reinforcement learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Observe–plan–act agent loops uses ReAct-style reasoning and acting as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
